expectations.text: Reads DeploymentMode (or equivalent) from config rather than hardcoding the mode â€” e.g. IsSaaS() helper or ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode")
expectations.evidence: IsSaaS() reads ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode") and compares string.Equals(mode, "SaaS", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase). Config key set in CXCustomizations.config.

expectations.text: Job row is always inserted into the job table (cx_job or similar) regardless of SaaS vs OnPrem â€” both paths insert first for tracking/auditing
expectations.evidence: Step 5 Submit() shows: var jobDbKey = _commandFactory.InsertJob(...) called unconditionally before the if(isSaaS) branch. Comment explicitly says 'Always insert the job â€” both modes'.

expectations.text: On the SaaS branch, the process proc is called immediately in the same web request (synchronous path) â€” not deferred to a service
expectations.evidence: if (isSaaS) { _commandFactory.ProcessJob(jobDbKey); } immediately after InsertJob. ProcessJob calls cx_pog_copy_process stored proc in-process.

expectations.text: On the OnPrem branch, only the insert happens â€” processing is deferred to a background service/Automator that polls the job table
expectations.evidence: Comment: '// OnPrem: return immediately; Automator/service polls cx_job'. No ProcessJob call on the OnPrem path. Process proc's NULL path designed for the background service.

expectations.text: Control implements IPopupControlSubscriber (not a custom popup â€” uses the built-in explorer)
expectations.evidence: Class declaration: 'public partial class MyControlUI : UserControlBase, IPopupControlSubscriber, ICommandManager'. Receive() method implemented.

expectations.text: Constructor subscribes to PublishedKey.JsDBKeyList (the multi-select result key)
expectations.evidence: Constructor has SubscriptionManager.AddKeyToSubscribe(PublishedKey.JsDBKeyList) alongside HierarchyDBKeyList and DBStatusList.

expectations.text: Popup opened with PageKey from GetPageKey("CKB Multi-select Planogram Explorer") â€” exact page name used
expectations.evidence: var pageKey = _commandFactory.GetPageKey("CKB Multi-select Planogram Explorer"); used in PlanogramList_ButtonClick.

expectations.text: Receive() method splits the returned keys by '#' delimiter (e.g. JsDBKeyList is '#'-delimited, not comma or space)
expectations.evidence: .Split(new char[] { '#' }, StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries) in the JsDBKeyList case of Receive().

expectations.text: Explains that $(document).ready fails because the OA framework loads jQuery after the script files execute, so jQuery isn't available at script parse time
expectations.evidence: 'the OA framework loads jQuery after the page's <script> tags have already executed. At the moment your script file is parsed, jQuery is still undefined'

expectations.text: Recommends Sys.Application.add_load as the correct OA replacement for $(document).ready
expectations.evidence: Full section 'The Correct Pattern: Sys.Application.add_load' with complete code example using Sys.Application.add_load(function() { var $ = jQuery; ... }).

expectations.text: Mentions that a jQuery IIFE wrapper (e.g. (function($){...})(jQuery)) also fails for the same reason â€” not just the document.ready form
expectations.evidence: 'the (function($){...}(jQuery)) IIFE) both fail silently in OA controls' â€” explicitly covers both forms failing.

expectations.text: If mentioning code-behind script injection, recommends ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock rather than Page.ClientScript (which is silently ignored when ScriptManager is present)
expectations.evidence: Section 'Registering Script from Code-Behind': 'Do not use Page.ClientScript.RegisterClientScriptBlock â€” it is silently ignored when a ScriptManager is present'. Shows ScriptManager.RegisterClientScriptBlock.

expectations.text: Reads DeploymentMode (or equivalent) from config rather than hardcoding the mode â€” e.g. IsSaaS() helper or ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode")
expectations.evidence: IsSaaS() reads ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode"). Note: baseline agent read the existing repo source files (POGSplitUI, PogCopyVersion) to derive this answer.

expectations.text: Job row is always inserted into the job table (cx_job or similar) regardless of SaaS vs OnPrem â€” both paths insert first for tracking/auditing
expectations.evidence: Rule table states 'Always insert the job record regardless of mode'. InsertJob called before the if(isSaaS) branch in the example.

expectations.text: On the SaaS branch, the process proc is called immediately in the same web request (synchronous path) â€” not deferred to a service
expectations.evidence: if (isSaaS) { _commandFactory.ProcessJob(jobDbKey); } shown immediately after InsertJob. Label: '// SaaS only: process the job immediately in-process'.

expectations.text: On the OnPrem branch, only the insert happens â€” processing is deferred to a background service/Automator that polls the job table
expectations.evidence: '// On-prem: return immediately; background service will pick up the job'. Baseline read the existing codebase to find this pattern.
